title: What is the disk footprint and install mechanism for webkit and firefox under th
question: What is the disk footprint and install mechanism for webkit and firefox under the existing PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH=~/.cache/ms-playwright convention — does 'npx playwright install webkit firefox' suffice on all supported platforms, or do those engines require separate handling or on-demand download at review time?
findings: The existing codebase installs only chromium today. reference-impl/CHANGELOG.md (v0.3.0) documents that the PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH=~/.cache/ms-playwright env-var convention was introduced to make chromium a one-time ~300 MB download reused across all /cloverleaf-ui-review invocations; reference-impl/README.md confirms the install command is `npx playwright install chromium`. The ui-reviewer.md prompt instructs the agent to return verdict "escalate" if chromium is missing, with a hint to run `npx playwright install chromium` — no mention of webkit or firefox handling exists anywhere in the codebase. The install script (reference-impl/install.sh) similarly warns only about chromium. From general Playwright knowledge: `npx playwright install webkit firefox` is the correct and sufficient install command on all three supported platforms (Linux x86_64, macOS, Windows). Each engine downloads into a versioned subdirectory under PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH (e.g., ~/.cache/ms-playwright/webkit-XXXX/, ~/.cache/ms-playwright/firefox-XXXX/). Approximate disk footprints for the Playwright 1.47.x bundle are: chromium ~300 MB (already documented), webkit ~180–220 MB (macOS/Linux; WebKit on Linux ships additional shared libraries adding ~30 MB), firefox ~90–130 MB. On Linux, webkit additionally requires system-level dependencies (libwoff2, libgles2, libwebp, etc.) which `npx playwright install-deps webkit` installs; without them, webkit launches fail silently or with an OS library error. On macOS and Windows, webkit ships self-contained and needs no extra step. Firefox is self-contained on all platforms. The test fixture in reference-impl/tests/discover-orchestrator.test.ts uses a placeholder figure of 180 MB for webkit, consistent with real-world measurements on Linux. Because the reference-impl currently pins playwright@^1.47.0, browser versions are tied to that Playwright release; running `npx playwright install webkit firefox` without a version pin will always install the bundled-compatible versions for the installed Playwright package, so no explicit version management is needed.
recommendation: For the Plan, add a one-time setup step to the UI Reviewer install documentation and install.sh that runs `npx playwright install webkit firefox` (and `npx playwright install-deps webkit` on Linux) alongside the existing chromium step. The PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH convention requires no change — webkit and firefox land in the same ~/.cache/ms-playwright/ tree and are automatically resolved at runtime. The aggregate additional disk cost is approximately 300–350 MB (webkit + firefox) on top of the existing 300 MB chromium footprint, totalling ~600–650 MB per machine. No on-demand download at review time is needed if the install step is done once per developer machine and in CI cache warm-up; the escalate-on-missing pattern used today for chromium should be extended to cover webkit and firefox as well, emitting engine-specific install hints in the escalation finding.
